module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency — 'I know how to test, I know how to learn, I know how to encode.' Not confidence-as-performance; competence-as-posture.

trace.persona: mid-level software engineer, 5 years experience, hands-on with Claude Code daily for 2 weeks, has shipped one agent to a non-critical workflow
trace.module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency — 'I know how to test, I know how to learn, I know how to encode.'

phases.phase_name: Whole module body (no ## Phase headers — module shell)
phases.persona_action: Reads the module page. Arrives with M5 packaged run in good shape; skips rescue blockquotes (not needed). Reads 'Start here' framing — 'You walk in holding two runs...' — and immediately connects to their own M4/M5 artefacts. Clicks through to the two openers, then the main exercise. Returns to Key Concepts to consolidate vocabulary. Reads Human close and sign-off; feels the practitioner-fluency landing. Module closes; ready to enter exercise.
confusion_flags: Key Concepts bullet 3 — 'Verifier when deterministic, judge when LLM-based, gate when placed in CI' — correct vocabulary, but the persona has only used 'verifier' informally before; the evals-as-family framing lands slightly ahead of where the exercise has reached at module-read time. Resolved when closing lectures ('the-loop-has-a-name') deliver the full framing after the exercise — placement is correct by design.
phases.artifact_state: Persona has both runs in hand. Module shell navigates them into the exercise and lectures. Second authored skill at ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md by module close; arc retrospective note at student-chosen location.
phases.mood_note: 'You walk in holding two runs of the same task.' Peer-flat, problem-state-setting. TEACHING beat. Contracts practitioner fluency from the first sentence. Score 8/10.

close.final_artifact: Second authored skill at ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md; ranked gap map (memory / verifier / skill); arc retrospective note (student-chosen repo location); at least one rule deleted from CLAUDE.local.md (compound-by-subtraction).
close.mood_note: 'Three runs of the loop on your own work. The kit, yours.' + Antti sign-off. Practitioner-fluency contract delivered. Competence-as-posture, not credibility-performance. Score 9/10.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — practitioner fluency is M6's contracted end-state; does not pre-empt M7 (awe + ownership).
Big Idea leads with discipline, not failure mode.
Strategy-fidelity — file matches M6 arc in strategy doc.
Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bullet 1 → theme #4 (grain-of-salt; artefact rules). Bullet 2 → theme #2 (compounding; gaps teach the next turn). Bullet 3 → theme #1 (90% correct; evals are the catch infrastructure). Bullets 4-5 → theme #2 (encoding is the loop). All five bullets instance at least one recurring theme.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — §§ 3/4/5 gates run first; breadcrumbs on survivors.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All five bullets pass §3 (discipline-lead) and §4 (no front-running; evals vocab is M6's designated beat per strategy doc). Breadcrumbs on bullets 1 and 5 are theme-essence hooks in engineer register. No standalone 'Themes this module instances:' section present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text. No authored conversation cues.
Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module shell has no fenced prompt blocks. Forcing functions are delegated to the linked exercise file. Module body frames mood and takeaways only.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 78-81: three named falsifiable outcomes (second skill at ~/.claude/skills/, rule deletion visible in git history, arc retrospective from artefacts not training memory). Arc-mood carve-out applied (names artefact + use, not activity).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — produced files named with stable identifiers in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 83-90: four artefact rows with stable identifiers (two-run gap map, stale-rule deletion, session-shaper skill at ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md, arc retrospective at student-picked location). Producing prompt and consuming modules named per row.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure modes + escape hatches present for every forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 92-99: seven failure-mode + escape-hatch rows across all exercise phases.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module structural-failure rescue callouts at module open when prior module send-off can fail.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 25-27: two consecutive blockquotes at ## Start here. First names rescue moves (nudge / re-send fresh / skip opener). Second names accept-loss floor ('Despair not'). Maintainer block line 71 documents the deliberate rule-5 exception.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers; ceiling-showing / arming lectures can open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Closing lectures (the-loop-has-a-name, agents-that-build-agents) correctly placed after exercises in ## Human close. Openers (the-2-frontiers, story-of-module-6) are ceiling-showing / permission-giving respectively. Strategy doc explicitly rationales story-of-module-6 placement: 'Goes first because permission works before the work, not after.'

Rule 2 targets contrast-mood modules (M5-style). M6's mood is practitioner fluency/leverage — not a contrast-mood module. Two opener lectures (ceiling-showing + permission-giving) before main exercise is within dosage for this mood contract.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — two-run gap diagnosis + second-skill authoring reliably triggers across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment is primarily carried by the linked exercise file. Module shell sets up the problem-state ('two runs of the same task') and delivers the vocabulary frame (Key Concepts). A student with both runs in hand and the module framing in memory enters the exercise with the correct problem-posture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — module matches strategy doc M6 Big Idea.
rules_evaluated.evidence: File Big Idea matches strategy doc M6 section ('Two runs in the pocket... Encoding is the third phase of test → learn → encode'). All three moves named: name the gap, pick the home, author the skill.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off correctly.
rules_evaluated.evidence: ## Next (line 60): 'On Monday, pick a task your team is sitting on, send it off packaged, read the return through the lens you just built.' M6 is final core module; bridges to real-world practice. Optional M7 entry-point present via agents-that-build-agents lecture.
